videos.title: AI Is transforming science — but does it understand any of it? | with Claire Malone
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description:
What does it mean to make a scientific discovery — and can a machine really do it?
📅 Filmed at the Ri on 2 May 2026

videos.title: Unveiling the ghost particle: Neutrinos and their impact on particle physics - with Kirsty Duffy
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description: Neutrinos are the most important particle you were never taught about. They are created in radioactive decays in the sun, the centre of the Earth, supernovae, and even beer. Particle physicist Kirsty Duffy joins us this month to discuss the origins and properties of neutrinos, as well as her work on

videos.title: Did you know that animals can laugh? Why laughter is important for animal social circuits #science
videos.author: The Royal Institution
videos.description:
Animals don't just play-fight: they enjoy being tickled, and even tell jokes! 🐁

Neurologist Kelly Clancy illustrates the important role play has in building social circuits.
